_notes: Generic environment configuration for the Smart Cities - Applications Screening domain. Defines agents, affordances, field mappings, and metric computation rules. Batch-specific statistics (P95, means, distributions) are NOT embedded here — they are derived at runtime from the logs.

pilot.name: Smart Cities — Applications Screening
pilot.description: Citizens submit applications via the Novoville platform. An AI screener evaluates each application for field-level errors. A human operator reviews AI-rejected or AI-flagged cases and issues the final decision. The three-event structure (citizen → AI → operator) is the collaboration unit.

agents.label: Citizen Applicant
agents.description: End-user submitting an application. Initiates the collaboration loop. No review or decision role.

agents.label: AI Document Screener
agents.description: AI model that evaluates each application for field-level errors and issues an initial screening decision.

agents.label: Municipal Operator
agents.description: Domain expert who reviews AI-rejected or AI-flagged applications and issues the final administrative decision. Primary human collaborator for HAIC metric computation.

task.name: application_screening
task.description: Each unit of work is one application record. The collaboration task is the full lifecycle from citizen submission through AI screening to (conditional) operator review.

affordances.label: Application submitted
affordances.description: Citizen submits a new application. Always the first event. Marks the start of the session timeline.

affordances.label: AI screens application
affordances.description: AI model completes field-level screening. `latency_ms` is the model response time. Operator review is triggered only when the AI decision is Rejected or Flagged for verification.

affordances.label: Operator reviews application
affordances.condition: Emitted only when ai_decision in ['Rejected', 'Flagged for verification']
affordances.description: Operator issues final decision on an AI-rejected or AI-flagged case. `duration_s` is the operator's active review time — the primary input for EL and HCL. `correct` labels the decision quality and is required for Tr computation.

EL.note: Baseline is auto-derived as P95 of `duration_s` across all `operator_verified` events in the batch (requires ≥10 observations). Set `baseline_override` and `rt_max_override` to fix explicit values when needed.

Tr.note: Tr returns None when no `correct`-labeled decisions exist in the batch. Apply `correct_derivation_rules` at ingestion time (e.g. in the Node-RED transform node) to populate the field before logging.

F.note: All three action types count toward F. F = total events / session_duration_minutes.

HCL.note: HCL = 1 - (duration_s / rt_max), averaged over operator_verified events. rt_max is shared with EL.

D.note: D is computed separately per actor_type. AI latency_ms is converted to seconds before averaging.

session_schema.note: Session start is the timestamp of `application_created`. Session end is the timestamp of `operator_verified` if present, otherwise `ai_evaluated`. This defines the T_session used in F computation.

node_red_mapping.description: Maps pilot log fields to the HAIC platform /logs/register payload. Each affordance action maps to a HAIC event type. The `meta.task_parameters` block must be included in every session POST.

meta_task_parameters.note: Set `baseline_s` and `rt_max` at runtime (from EL config above) or leave null for auto-derivation.

fairness.note: Fairness evaluation requires demographic subgroup labels not present in the current application schema.

surrogate_similarity.note: S metric requires surrogate_action and surrogate_probs fields. Not applicable for real-world pilot logs.
